def: To give in and take a more accommodating position.
Skilful and tactful in handling people or delicate matters.
Legally binding; also dependable and cordial.
A formal declaration of what one plans to do.
An action taken to deal with a problem or reach a goal.
A description of a possible course of events.
By means of the action described; through the fact that.
As a logical result; therefore.
Affecting or relating to a whole system rather than its parts.
A close linking of things so they influence one another.
The unfair difference in status, wealth, or opportunity.
Government policy aimed at helping poorer countries develop.
The questioning of the effects of worldwide economic integration.
The ability to interact effectively across cultures.
In spite of that; all the same.
To deny or challenge a claim; also to cover the cost of something.
Affecting or covering the whole world.
Extending across national borders.
A political style that claims to speak for ordinary people against elites.
The courage to stand up for what is right in public.
A direct vote by the people on a single question.
A false or misleading picture of something.
The division of government into law-making, executive, and judicial parts.
The right of the media to report without government control.
To rely on something or someone in order to manage.
Able and ready to defend itself, as a robust democracy.
Standing for or typical of a larger group.
The way a company is directed and controlled.
The aim of making the largest possible profit.
The use of machines to do work once done by people.
A company's duty to act in the interests of society and the environment.
The full sequence of steps that add value to a product.
The whole chain of steps that brings a product from source to buyer.
To direct or shape something toward a particular aim.
To take no notice of something on purpose.
Concerning or lasting only a short time; also at short notice.
Concerning or lasting over a long period.
The ability of machines to perform tasks that normally need human thought.
The conversion of processes and data into digital form.
The protection of data against loss or unauthorised access.
A description of a possible future situation.
A regular task done in the same way each time.
To take responsibility or control of something; also to adopt.
To hold something within, such as a hidden risk; also to rescue.
Large enough to have a real effect; substantial.
Not held in check or supervised.
The set of qualities that make a person or group who they are.
The state of being a member of a group or place.
The way a person sees and judges themselves.
The dominance of one group or state over others.
The way a topic is discussed and framed in society.
The written story of a person's life.
To think deeply and carefully about something.
Able to use, or written in, several languages.
Using or knowing only one language.
The central bank's main rate, which guides all other rates.
The ease with which assets can be turned into cash.
A major shift in the direction of interest rates.
The tendency of prices to change sharply and often.
Something of value that a person or company owns.
A decline in economic activity.
To spread investments so that losses in one are offset by others.
A rising market versus a falling one.
To buy something now and pay for it later.
The official updating of one's registered address.
A landlord's form confirming that a tenant has moved in.
The official record of who owns land and property.
An organisation owned and run jointly by its members.
To pay a sum held as security, for example when renting.
An AI system trained to understand and produce language.
The capacity of a computer to process data quickly.
The written instructions that make up a computer program.
The examples used to teach a machine-learning model.
Software whose source code is freely available to all.
To handle data instantly as it arrives.
A proposed law put before a parliament for debate.
The shift from fossil fuels to renewable energy sources.
The share of eligible people who actually vote.
The carbon dioxide released into the air, mainly by burning fuel.
Money given by the state to support an industry or activity.
The shift toward cleaner, more sustainable transport.
In a way that burdens the people who come after us.
A serious dealing with a topic; also a dispute.
The set of facts of a particular matter.
A particular aspect or point of view.
One particular part or feature of something.
The way a text or talk is arranged into parts.
To draw special attention to something.
To make something clearer or easier to understand.
To make something clear by giving detail.
To place things side by side to compare them.
To state the main points briefly.
On the other hand; however.
In a way that fits; as a result.
Having a major, guiding influence.
The making of laws; also the laws made.
The power exercised by the state.
The involvement of citizens in public decisions.
The period during which someone holds an official post.
A change made to improve a system or law.
The greater number; more than half.
A formal discussion where opposing views are argued.
To formally approve a law; also to say goodbye.
To control something by rules; also to settle a matter.
Causing strong disagreement among people.
Something valuable gained through effort, especially by society.
A weakening or harming of something's function or quality.
A difference between things that should match.
The state of having mixed or conflicting feelings.
The quality of fitting together in a clear, logical way.
The quality of seeming reasonable or believable.
The full right of a state to govern itself.
The use of resources in a way that does not harm the future.
The way two things affect each other.
One of the general conditions that shape what is possible.
The freedom to act or decide within limits.
How far something extends or how many it affects.
The importance or value given to something.
The extent of something's importance or consequences.
The size or scale of something.
The particular way of tackling a task.
The manner in which one sets about a problem.
A situation where the rules are unclear or ambiguous.
A difficult course between two risks or extremes.
The quality of being certain to happen and impossible to avoid.
The quality of being random or interchangeable, without firm criteria.
General agreement among a group.
A firm, hopeful belief that things will go well.
A calm, unruffled state of mind.
The quality of continuing firmly despite difficulty.
